mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates signal extreme monetary accommodation that compresses discount rates and lowers the cost of capital, supporting equity prices as long as the ZLB persists.
failure_condition: Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur less than 55% of the time when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below the post-crisis peak signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer confidence and spending, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In quarters where UNRATE declines below 9.0%, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is not significantly greater than 50% (binomial test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales reflect broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate earnings and lifts equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0 and HOUST month-over-month growth > 0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: When both proxies rise for two consecutive months, the median 60-day SPX return is not positive or the proportion of negative returns exceeds 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an early economic expansion indicates strengthening manufacturing activity and supply-side momentum that supports equity prices through improved corporate profits.
failure_condition: In the early expansion regime (real GDP growth positive but below potential), the correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend real GDP growth signals a resilient economy that boosts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, generating positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When GDP growth exceeds 2% annualized, the proportion of positive 60-day SPX returns is below 60% or the average excess return over non-trigger periods is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero policy rates combined with rising CPI indicate reflation that reduces real debt burdens and boosts nominal earnings, leading to positive equity returns over a one-month horizon; falling CPI in this environment signals deflation risk and negative returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, the average 20-day SPX return is not positive or the return distribution is not stochastically greater than baseline (one-sided MWW test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls directly signals improving labor demand, rising incomes, and consumer spending, which translate into positive corporate earnings and equity returns.
failure_condition: The monthly change in PAYEMS shows no positive correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns (pearson r <= 0 or p > 0.10) over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts in the early recovery from a housing bust signal firming demand in the residential sector, which boosts construction employment and household wealth, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: During the early recovery regime (housing starts below long-term average but rising), the 5-day forward return after positive monthly HOUST readings is not positive in at least 55% of cases.
